questions.question: Explain the difference between printf() and scanf() functions in C with examples.
keyPoints: printf() is for OUTPUT, scanf() is for INPUT
printf() writes formatted data to stdout
scanf() reads formatted data from stdin
Both use format specifiers like %d, %f, %c, %s
scanf() requires address-of operator (&) for variables

questions.question: What are format specifiers in C? List four commonly used format specifiers with their meaning.
keyPoints: Format specifiers begin with % and tell printf/scanf data type
%d - integer (signed decimal)
%f - floating point number
%c - single character
%s - string of characters

questions.question: What is the purpose of escape sequences in C? Give two examples.
keyPoints: Escape sequences represent special non-printable characters
They begin with backslash (\)
\n - newline, moves cursor to next line
\t - horizontal tab
\a - alert/bell sound

questions.question: Why is gets() considered unsafe? What is the recommended alternative?
keyPoints: gets() does not check buffer boundaries
It can cause buffer overflow leading to security vulnerabilities
It reads until newline without limit
Recommended alternative is fgets() which accepts maximum characters
fgets(char[], size, stdin) is safer

questions.question: What is the purpose of the & (address-of) operator in scanf()? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: & obtains the memory address of a variable
scanf() needs location to store input value
Without &, scanf() would use garbage address
Example: scanf("%d", &num) stores input at num's address
For strings (arrays), & is not needed

questions.question: Differentiate between formatted and unformatted I/O functions in C.
keyPoints: Formatted I/O allows data format control using specifiers
printf() and scanf() are formatted functions
Unformatted I/O reads/writes raw data without format conversion
getchar(), putchar(), gets(), puts() are unformatted
Formatted functions offer more control but are slower

questions.question: Write a C program to input your name and age, and display them formatted.
keyPoints: Use scanf() with %s for name string
Use scanf() with %d for age integer
Use printf() with format specifiers to display
Include proper prompts using printf()
Add newline escape sequences for readability

questions.question: Write a C program to perform arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) on two numbers entered by the user. Display the results with appropriate messages.
keyPoints: Declare variables for two numbers and results
Use printf() to display prompts
Use scanf() to read both numbers with %lf for double
Calculate all four operations
Handle division by zero case
Display results with printf() using %.2f for precision
Use appropriate newline characters

questions.question: Explain the working of printf() function with respect to format specifiers. Include in your answer the purpose of field width and precision specifiers with examples.
keyPoints: printf() takes format string and variable arguments
Format specifiers start with % and define data type
Field width: %5d prints integer in 5-character wide field
Precision: %.2f prints float with 2 decimal places
Width can be left-aligned using negative sign: %-5d
Combining: %8.2f means 8-wide with 2-decimal precision

questions.question: Write a C program that converts temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit and vice versa. The program should accept temperature value and conversion choice from user.
keyPoints: Use printf() for user prompts
Use scanf() with %f for float temperature
Use integer variable for conversion choice (1 or 2)
Formula: F = (C * 9/5) + 32
Formula: C = (F - 32) * 5/9
Display result with appropriate formatting
Handle invalid choice gracefully

questions.question: Discuss common errors that occur while using scanf() function. How can they be prevented?
keyPoints: Missing & operator for non-array variables
Using wrong format specifier for data type
Not clearing input buffer after numeric input
Reading newline character with %c
Using gets() instead of fgets() for strings
Leaving format specifier white space unhandled
Solutions: Use & correctly, match specifiers, use getchar() or fflush() to clear buffer
